feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: V.02 incomplete document
entry.content: Need to complete sales orders which have incompleteness with Shipping point and net value as the feilds are greyed out in VA02 so we are not able to complete the orders.

entry.title: Maintain additional details in customer-info material records in Classify
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement to maintain additional details/two customer information records in the VD51 transaction. But SAP doesn't allow two customer material info records for the same material, and we can only add the customer material (KNMT-KDMAT) and customer description (KNMT-POSTX).

Therefore, we maintained the additional details using classify; by creating a class and characteristics, and let me know how this is helpful to you. Also, share your thoughts on this method and any corrections required or any different approach we can use.

Go to Transaction VD51 and enter the customer, sales org., and dist. channel.

Now, enter the material for which we have to maintain the customer material description and add additional details.

Select the line time and click on the Classify button.

Here we have to assign the class: CUST_MAT with class type 052 (customer-material info record)

The custom class for the additional customer material number is CUST_MAT.

Characteristic Value assigned in CUST_MAT Class: -

Fetch details using tables.

Concatenate the sales org + distribution channel + customer + material and pass into table INOB with class type (KLART) 052 and table (OBTAB) KNMT and copy object No. CUOBJ.

Now pass the CUOBJ number into the AUSP table where OBJEK = CUOBJ and fetch ATWRT.

Assignment of Characteristic Type to Class

Characteristic Type Creation CUST_MAT2 Customer Material 2 Created in CT04 and assigned the characteristic type to the class CUST_MAT in CL02.

Maybe help https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/39d6d7a325d04826b95328b25fad91ab/b9cdc353b677b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US


https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/two-on-one-customer-material/qaq-p/6220339

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/sales-order-item-description-coming-from-customer-material-info-record-cmir/qaq-p/9521256

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/use-same-customer-material-record-for-many-other-customers/qaq-p/10806131

626931 - FAQ: Customer material info record

Regards,
Lovepreet Sidhu

entry.title: Exchange rate issue
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

We are implementing India Localization and facing issue related to Exchange rate.

When we are creating Proforma Invoice may be on date 01.06.2026 and exchange rate as M type is maintained that day if it was 90 and after post goods issue we created ZF2 billing type may be on 15.06.2026 and exchange rate changed to 92 then we will have issue in Tax assesable value.

As per requirement we want to prevent it and get exchange rate to be copied from proforma invoice at header level.

is there any way to prevent this as part of India requirement that exchange rate copied from proforma Invoice

entry.title: Material V-GW_PREMIUM does not exist in storage location 2001 VO
entry.content:
Hi,

After upgrade to EHP8 from EHP0 in PDP/QDP,  a Service material V-GW_PREMIUM is giving error that it's not extended to a storage location. where item category is TAX.

For this material General plant data/Storage 1 isn't maintained before or after upgrade but after upgrade we face the error while creating the material

but the same material we are able to create a order in QDX (non upgraded sandbox) this material doesn't have the entry in MARD.

entry.title: Table S066 is not updating automatically when we are creating sales order through EDI in SAP ECC
entry.content:
Issue: Table S066 is not updating automatically when we are creating sales order through EDI in SAP ECC.  But the table S066 is updating automatically when we are creating sales orders through VA01 and also when we are editing the sales order through VA02 which was created through EDI.
Expected solution: Table S066 should get update automatically when we are creating SO through EDI.
Can anyone help us to overcome the issue please?

@Lakshmipathi @sravan_aleshwaram @HenrikeGroetecke - Please provide your insights?
Thanking you in Advance!

entry.title: Idoc error
entry.content: On an intercompany invoice idoc errors and says delivery does not exist

entry.title: Hiding Condition type
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Our client's requirements is to not to show the inventory price of the Material to the Salesman. While in the Sales quotation and Sales order Pricing procedure the PCIP Condition type displays the Internal Price which is used to calculate the Profit margin of the Material. This Profit margin value is used in the SAP Standard sales reports because of this I can't remove this condition type from the Pricing procedure, so i want to hide this conditon type in the screen without removing its function in the Sales quotation and Sales order. Please provide me the possible way to achive this requirement or suggest any workaround for this requirement.

Best Regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: Price Group Condition ZA00 not working
entry.content: P80-658: group Pricing not working ZA00 condition

entry.title: Freight Condition ZHD0 not distributing to all lines on Invoice
entry.content:
We have a Freight Condition getting updated on delivery header directly . It is coming from interface.

When the invoice is created with reference to delivery, the entire freight value sits on the last time of the invoice. (Please note I am not referring to rounding value but entire freight value)

When the credit memo is created only for the last time, then seems like we are giving the entire freight value back to customer instead of the proportionate value, which is wrong and when credit memo is created for other lines, then there is no credit given to customer for freight. 

However, when we put  Freight condition type on the sales order, then we see that it distributes the freight across all lines. Same information is passed to delivery/invoice. 

And when the credit memo is created for a specific line item, then the proportionate freight is given to customer, which is right. 

I dont think so there is a config issue with condition type as it is same being used on order & delivery. 

So, can you please confirm if this is how SAP Standard works and if there is a way to distribute the freight on all the lines on the invoice if the freight is polulated on delivery directly.

entry.title: Minimize Custom condition types and account keys for REA1, RES2, REA5 etc. in settlement Management
entry.content:
Hello CCM Experts,

We have requirement to create custom condition types for different price promotions. There are around 26 conditions types in sales rebate we have created in place of RES1 so that each condition will be assigned to unique G/L Account.

Now for REA1, RES2, REA5 do we need to create 26 custom promotional conditions for each, as they are applicable for all promotional conditions types in accrual and reversal and we should also create custom account keys. This is making pricing procedure complex. 

Our aim is to assign each promotion condition to a specific G/L Account .

Could you please suggest alternativees to possibly reduce to one account keys for all these reversals.

Regards 

Kiran Kumar Dara 

Settlement Management  SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing  SD Sales

entry.title: Need to complete Sales order inVA02
entry.content:
Hello Team,
Need to complete sales orders which have incompleteness with Shipping point and net value as the feilds are greyed out in VA02 so we are not able to complete the orders.So many incomplete orders are there in V.02 since 2006 and it is affecting the performance of the report.
Could you please suggest further.

entry.title: error transaction INTRASALES- SAP
entry.content:
Business Scenario

T Code VEFU

During Intrastat reporting, we have scenarios where Credit Memo and Debit Memo billing documents need to be linked to the corresponding original invoice for reporting purposes. However, in certain cases, the material/article referenced in the Credit Memo or Debit Memo differs from the material/article in the original invoice.

As a result, the expected linkage between the adjustment document and the original invoice is not established in the Intrastat work file records.

Clarification Required

Could you please confirm:

  1. Does standard SAP ECC provide functionality to manually link a Credit Memo or Debit Memo to an original invoice for Intrastat reporting purposes when the material/article references are different? If yes, is there any standard transaction (e.g., VEFU or any other Foreign Trade / Intrastat transaction) that allows maintenance of the following Intrastat reference fields:
    • IREFBELEG (Reference Document)
    • IREFPOSIT (Reference Item)
    • IREFMONAT (Reference Month)
    • IREFEJAHR (Reference F

entry.title: SAP ECC PE1 – issue generating invoice from transfer order
entry.content:
Hi Team

User facing this error while TO in SAP, packaging shipment 224558105

entry.title: Error in storage location determination using VA01 creating with reference
entry.content:
We have the wrong storage location determined in the return sales order 0888057809  

As per the configuration it should be picked 1002 it got picked with 1000

entry.title: user can not delete the inbound delivery, it shows
entry.content:
user can not delete the inbound delivery, it shows the confirmation cannot be deleted

Message no. ME635

entry.title: Can the Tax Code be Edited in a Debit Memo Created with Reference to a Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a question regarding the standard behavior in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

When creating a Debit Memo with reference to a Sales Order, I noticed that the Tax Code is not editable.

I would like to confirm the following:

  1. Is there any standard configuration or business process that allows the Tax Code to be changed manually in the Debit Memo?
  2. If manual editing is not supported, what is the recommended SAP approach when a different tax code is required for the Debit Memo?

Any insights or references to SAP documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance !

Best regards, 

HY

entry.title: Storage Location Field Not Populating via Inbound ORDERS IDoc (ORDERS05) in QR1
entry.content:
We are currently experiencing an issue with sales order creation triggered by our external system. The inbound data is successfully received in SAP ECC via IDoc using message type ORDERS and basic type ORDERS05 in QR1.

Issue Description:
Although the storage location information is correctly populated within the incoming IDoc segments (as shown in the attached screenshot), the Storage Location (LGORT) field remains blank on the generated sales order line items.

 

To help us diagnose the root cause, could you please verify the following areas:

  • Segment Mapping: Confirm if the storage location value from the external system is mapped to the correct IDoc field E1EDP01 - LGORT

Could you please investigate this behavior and let us know why the storage location is failing to update?

entry.title: Clarification on Standard Processing of Multiple DELFOR IDocs
entry.content:
The sender system (Infineon) and the receiver system (Bosch) are both SAP systems.

Infineon sends Supplier Confirmations for the same Scheduling Agreement in two separate DELFOR IDocs (one containing Firm schedule lines and another containing Forecast schedule lines) in DELFOR D.97A/D.04A format.

We would like to understand the standard SAP behavior for processing these multiple inbound DELFOR IDocs. Specifically, how should SAP process Supplier Confirmations received in separate IDocs for the same Scheduling Agreement.

Expectation:

 

Is there any standard SAP functionality to merge or aggregate multiple inbound DELFOR IDocs into a single Supplier Confirmation before updating the Scheduling Agreement (EKES)? If yes, please provide the standard program, function module, class, or processing logic responsible for this scenario.

 

If no such standard functionality exists, please advise the recommended SAP standard approach for processing Supplier Confirmations received in multiple DELFOR IDocs for the same Scheduling Agreement.

entry.title: VA05 Report getting duplicate entries
entry.content:
Dear Team,

in VA05 Standard report ,We are getting  Duplicate entries.

due to this duplicate entries we are not getting proper report.

Thanks

entry.title: Condition contract (WCOCO) triggers error "Field KVGR1 of condition table A9DS is not supported"
entry.content: We have created new condition table 9DS in the (V/03) with the standard fields key combination COCO_NUM,KVGR1,KVGR2, KONDM,LAND1,REGIO for the condition type YS01 and for the condition contract type YR01, when we create the condition contract system triggers the error message "Field KVGR1 of condition table A9DS is not supported"
